edus.text: Former Vice President Joe Biden , 2020 Democratic presidential candidate , smiles during an primary night rally in the Baldwin Hills neighborhood of Los Angeles , California , U.S. , on Tuesday , March 3 , 2020 .
WAS
INGTON -
resumptive Democratic presidential nominee Joe Biden holds a double-digit lead nationally over President Donald Trump ,
ith 7 in 10 voters saying
he country is on the wrong track ,
nd majorities disapproving of the president 's handling of the coronavirus and race relations .
hose are the major findings of a new national NBC News/Wall Street Journal poll
hat comes three and a half months before the 2020 presidential election , amid a pandemic
hat has killed approximately 140,000 Americans ,
nd during protests and debate over race across the country .
he poll shows Biden ahead of Trump by 11 points among registered voters , 51 percent to 40 percent ,
hich is well outside the poll 's margin of error of plus or minus 3.3 percentage points .
iden 's lead in last month 's poll was 7 points , 49 percent to 42 percent .
n addition , the poll shows Democrats enjoying an intensity advantage
eading into November ,
nd it has Trump 's job rating declining to 42 percent
its lowest level in two years .
` The atmosphere and the attitudes toward Donald Trump are the most challenging
n incumbent president has faced since Jimmy Carter in 1980 and Lyndon Johnson in 1968 , ''
aid Democratic pollster Peter Hart ,
hose firm conducted the survey with Republican pollster Bill McInturff of Public Opinion Strategies .
ut the survey also finds silver linings for Trump and the GOP , as well as warning signs for Biden and the Democrats :
majority of voters approve of the president 's handling of the economy ;
iden 's personal ratings have dropped since last month ;
nd Democrats no longer hold a double-digit lead in congressional preference .
` As Americans are coping with the coronavirus and a reckoning on race , politically ,
here is a great deal of sorting going on , ''
dded Democratic pollster Jeff Horwitt of Hart Research Associates .
` We are a long ways away from knowing
ow this will all play out in voters ' minds . ''
n his matchup against Trump , Biden 's biggest advantages are with African American voters
80 percent to 6 percent ) ;
atinos
67-22 percent ) ;
oters ages 18 to 34
62-23 percent ) ;
omen
58-35 percent ) ;
nd white voters with college degrees
53-38 percent ) .
rump is ahead among all white voters
49 percent to 42 percent ) ,
en
45-43 percent )
nd whites without college degrees
57-35 percent ) .
nd in the combined 11 battleground states
Arizona , Colorado , Florida , Maine , Michigan , Minnesota , Nevada , New Hampshire , North Carolina , Pennsylvania and Wisconsin -
iden leads Trump by 12 points , 52 percent to 40 percent .
hat 's more , half of voters
50 percent -
ay
here is no chance at all
hey would vote for Trump
compared with 37 percent
ho say the same of Biden ) .
nd in a separate question , a majority
52 percent -
ay
hey would be `` very uncomfortable ''
oting for Trump
compared with 38 percent
ho say that of Biden ) .
